# @zakkster/lite-headless / password-input

A password field with a show/hide toggle. Flips the input's `type` between
"password" and "text", keeps the toggle's aria-pressed / aria-label in sync,
and links the two with aria-controls.

## Quick example

```js
import { createPasswordInput } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/password-input";

const p = createPasswordInput({ visible: false });
p.attachInput(inputEl);    // controls input.type
p.attachToggle(buttonEl);  // click toggles; aria-pressed reflects state

p.show();   // type="text"
p.hide();   // type="password"
p.toggle();
p.isVisible(); // boolean
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `visible` | `false` | Start with the value revealed |
| `onVisibilityChange` | -- | `(visible: boolean) => void` |

## API

```ts
{
    isVisible(): boolean
    setVisible(v: boolean): void
    toggle(): void
    show(): void
    hide(): void

    attachInput(el): off
    attachToggle(el): off
    attachRoot(el): off

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

- input: `data-password-input`, `type="password|text"`, `data-visible`
- toggle: `data-password-toggle`, `aria-pressed`, `aria-controls="<input id>"`, `aria-label` ("Show password"/"Hide password", if not pre-set), `data-visible`, `type="button"` (if a `<button>`)
- root: `data-password-input-root`, `data-visible`

The input's original `type` is restored on detach. The input id is
auto-generated only if the toggle is attached and the input has no id.

## Wrapper (`<lite-password-input>`)

```html
<lite-password-input>
  <input data-input type="password" autocomplete="current-password" />
  <button data-toggle>show</button>
</lite-password-input>
```

Reactive: the `visible` attribute flows through bidirectionally.
Host: `toggle()`, `show()`, `hide()`, `setVisible(v)`, `visible`.
